oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Case-based fluid therapy in calves and ancillary fluid therapy in cows
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Balog, Blake Jordan
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Fluid therapy is a cornerstone of treatment for dehydrated and acidemic cattle, yet optimal protocols require careful patient assessment and individualized treatment plans. This article presents a case-based approach to fluid therapy in neonatal calves with diarrhea and adult cattle with various metabolic derangements. Common presentations include neonatal calf diarrhea, abomasal atony, and ruminal acidosis, each requir­ing specific therapeutic interventions. Assessment of hydration status using eyeball recession, skin tent duration, and core-to-peripheral temperature differences guides the decision be­tween oral and intravenous fluid therapy. Acidemia assessment through clinical signs and venous blood pH measurements determines bicarbonate requirements. Four clinical cases il­lustrate practical treatment protocols: mild dehydration with moderate acidemia, severe dehydration with sepsis, left dis­placed abomasum with metabolic alkalosis, and carbohydrate overload with severe acidosis. Key treatment goals include expanding extracellular volume, correcting acidemia and elec­trolyte abnormalities, restoring energy deficits, and facilitating intestinal repair. Oral electrolyte solutions should be evaluated carefully for their composition to ensure they meet our treat­ment goals. Intravenous hypertonic solutions offer a unique opportunity to rapidly increase extracellular volume and rapid correction of blood pH the case of hypertonic bicarbonate. Iso­tonic fluids are the critical for replacement of large fluid deficits and care should be taken in selecting the appropriate solution, volume, and fluid rate for a case-by-case basis.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 7-12
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
